evidence.metrics: No user feedback provided; simulation executed successfully.

no-user-feedback-to-triage.error_identification: No user feedback was provided, so there are no user-prioritized blockers to triage.
no-user-feedback-to-triage.root_cause_analysis: N/A (missing input signal rather than a simulator fault).
no-user-feedback-to-triage.correct_approach: Add a lightweight run summary in stdout (e.g., top-3 worst categories by time-of-day JSD, overall missing-distance rate) to guide users to give targeted feedback next iteration.
no-user-feedback-to-triage.key_insight: When user feedback is absent, proactively emit interpretable diagnostics that directly map to blueprint metrics to accelerate the next iteration.

blueprint_refs: Output must be a single daily trajectory string EXACTLY matching 1921Y.json format.
outputs: "trajectory_string_outputs (per agent, per validation date; strict 1921Y.json format)"

evidence.metrics: Program writes multiple files including simulated_trajectories_validation.json; blueprint calls for output to be a single daily trajectory string matching 1921Y.json format.

noncompliant-output-format-not-single-19.error_identification: The pipeline produces a JSON wrapper with trajectories plus extra metadata/files, rather than emitting outputs that “EXACTLY” match the 1921Y.json schema of agent_id -> list[day_log strings] as the sole artifact.
noncompliant-output-format-not-single-19.root_cause_analysis: In main()/save_results(), outputs are split into multiple auxiliary files and the trajectories are nested under {format_spec, trajectories, meta}. This deviates from the blueprint requirement to output in the same format as 1921Y.json (top-level agent_id keys mapping to list of day_log strings) without additional wrapper fields.
noncompliant-output-format-not-single-19.correct_approach: Change the trajectory output writer to emit a single JSON file whose top-level object is exactly trajectories_by_agent (agent_id -> list[str]) with no extra keys. Keep auxiliary files if desired, but ensure the required primary output artifact is schema-identical to 1921Y.json.
noncompliant-output-format-not-single-19.key_insight: Treat schema-compatibility as a first-class contract: produce a dedicated ‘ground-truth-shaped’ artifact, and put diagnostics in separate files.

blueprint_refs: execution modes: calibrated generative sequential model or optional LLM pipeline for mobility

evidence.metrics: Execution succeeded without using these classes; they are unrelated to mobility and introduce external dependency paths.

disallowed-llm-review-pipeline-in-mobili.error_identification: The code includes an unrelated product-review LLM pipeline (MemoryAgent/PlanningAgent/ReviewAuthor) that is not part of the mobility simulator blueprint and increases maintenance/dependency risk.
disallowed-llm-review-pipeline-in-mobili.root_cause_analysis: These classes are dead code for the mobility task. They also add environment requirements (OPENAI_API_KEY, openai SDK) that are irrelevant and could cause future runs to fail if accidentally invoked.
disallowed-llm-review-pipeline-in-mobili.correct_approach: Remove the review-related classes and OpenAI helper functions from this mobility simulator module. If a shared repository requires them, move them to a separate module not imported/executed by the simulator entrypoint.
disallowed-llm-review-pipeline-in-mobili.key_insight: Keep simulation code minimal and blueprint-scoped; unrelated integrations should live behind separate entrypoints to avoid accidental coupling.

blueprint_refs: step_distance_km_distribution_distance: drop steps with missing coords.
Build reverse index keyed by full POI id 'Name#id' -> (lat, lon).

evidence.metrics: gt_step_distance_missing_count is very large in logs; trip_distance_wasserstein=0.3954 while many GT steps are dropped, making the distribution comparison potentially unrepresentative.

distance-missingness-skews-step-distance.error_identification: Step-distance comparison is computed on a heavily truncated ground-truth set due to missing POI coordinates, so the resulting Wasserstein distance may not reflect true step-length realism.
distance-missingness-skews-step-distance.root_cause_analysis: Evaluator.compute_metrics drops steps when env.distance_km returns None. With a high missingness rate for GT POIs (as indicated by the logged gt_step_distance_missing_count), the evaluated GT distribution is biased toward POIs that exist in poi_id_to_latlon, and can diverge from the actual validation distribution.
distance-missingness-skews-step-distance.correct_approach: Add a guardrail: if GT missingness exceeds a threshold (e.g., >30% of GT steps), report trip_distance_wasserstein but also (a) log missingness ratio prominently and (b) optionally compute a fallback proxy distance metric using category-level typical distances only if permitted. Within current blueprint constraints, at minimum incorporate missingness ratio into calibration objective by penalizing high sim missingness and reporting GT missingness as a warning, so the metric isn’t over-trusted.
distance-missingness-skews-step-distance.key_insight: Distance-based metrics are only meaningful when coordinate coverage is comparable; always quantify missingness and gate calibration/interpretation accordingly.

blueprint_refs: time_of_day_histogram_jsd_by_category: per-category start-time histograms; report average and per-category.

evidence.metrics: tod_jsd_avg=0.2064; per-category JSD is high for College & University (~0.54), Event (~0.52), Residence (~0.44).

poor-time-of-day-fit-in-several-categori.error_identification: Average time-of-day divergence is moderate, but several categories have large mismatches (notably College & University, Event, Residence).
poor-time-of-day-fit-in-several-categori.root_cause_analysis: Category selection is influenced by (a) priors.cat_prob, (b) transition probs, and (c) a fixed multiplier 1.25*log(tod_prob) in _choose_next_category, but the scheduler also clamps target times using active_window and a max_wait heuristic, and the POI choice adds lateness penalties that can shift start times away from category hist peaks. These interacting heuristics can distort the intended time-of-day distribution, especially for categories that are rare or tightly time-bound in data.
poor-time-of-day-fit-in-several-categori.correct_approach: Make time modeling explicit and less deformable: (1) sample target_start_minute from category histogram (already done), but (2) instead of clamping hard to active_window/max_wait, resample a limited number of times conditioned on feasibility, and (3) reduce downstream time shifts by selecting POI first with distance/utility, then setting start_time to the sampled target (with travel feasibility) rather than max(target, arrival) + extra clamping. Additionally, tune the tod logit multiplier as a calibratable parameter (within existing calibration framework) rather than a hardcoded 1.25.
poor-time-of-day-fit-in-several-categori.key_insight: If time-of-day is an evaluation target, avoid layered heuristics (clamps/late penalties) that silently re-shape the sampled time distribution; keep timing as a primary sampled variable with feasibility handled by resampling.

blueprint_refs: POI choice conditioned on category, distance, and agent preferences.
low-topk-poi-recall-indicates-weak-perso.error_identification: Simulated trajectories often fail to include an agent’s frequently visited POIs (only ~30% of GT top-10 POIs are visited at least once across validation days).
low-topk-poi-recall-indicates-weak-perso.root_cause_analysis: Two compounding causes: (1) priors.poi_prob is smoothed over the entire global POI universe (global_pois = all POIs with coordinates), which can be extremely large and dilutes probability mass away from the agent’s seen POIs; (2) _choose_poi uses large candidate pools (near + explore + category candidates), with only a modest repeat penalty and relatively small beta_distance in best params, enabling frequent selection of plausible but non-personal POIs within a category.
low-topk-poi-recall-indicates-weak-perso.correct_approach: Constrain personalization support: build priors.poi_prob over a finite support focused on the agent’s seen POIs plus a small exploration tail per category (e.g., seen_pois ∪ top-M nearest POIs to home per category). Keep smoothing within that support. In _choose_poi, increase the weight of personal affinity by adding an explicit boost for seen POIs (or calibrate an exploration rate that controls how often explore candidates are included). This stays within blueprint (preference + distance + transitions) but prevents global dilution.
low-topk-poi-recall-indicates-weak-perso.key_insight: When the item universe is huge, don’t smooth over the full global support; define a principled candidate/support set so preference learning remains identifiable and recall doesn’t collapse.

blueprint_refs: parameters: alpha_pref, alpha_transition; transition_model_params from TRAIN days.
policy: next-activity conditioned on transitions and time-of-day.

evidence.metrics: transition_divergence is low (0.0109), but this may be dominated by smoothing/backoff and doesn’t guarantee realistic sequences; other metrics (topk_poi_recall, tod_jsd_avg) remain weak.

transition-model-underweighted-by-constr.error_identification: Even with low transition_divergence, the simulator may be achieving this via heavy smoothing/backoff rather than genuinely learning sequential structure, while POI recall and time-of-day alignment remain poor.
transition-model-underweighted-by-constr.root_cause_analysis: In _choose_next_category and _choose_poi, transition probabilities are blended with preference using _transition_backoff_weight(smoothing_strength)=x/(1+x) but then clipped to [0.02,0.75]. With very small calibrated smoothing_strength (~1e-6), backoff becomes 0.02, which still injects preference mass into transitions; additionally alpha_pref/alpha_transition are forced to sum to 1 at sampling time, limiting calibration flexibility. The transition metric itself is computed as JSD over add-1 smoothed global bigram distributions, which can mask errors.
transition-model-underweighted-by-constr.correct_approach: Make backoff and TOD weighting calibratable rather than derived from smoothing_strength, and remove the hard minimum backoff of 0.02 (allow near-zero when data supports). Keep smoothing_strength strictly for count smoothing, not for behavioral mixing. Also consider computing transition_divergence without add-1 smoothing for diagnostics (while keeping a smoothed version for stability) so calibration can’t “win” by flattening distributions.
transition-model-underweighted-by-constr.key_insight: Don’t reuse ‘smoothing’ as a behavioral mixture control; separate statistical smoothing from decision-policy mixing to keep calibration interpretable.
